location.college_name: Visvesvaraya National Institute of Technology, Nagpur
location.nearest_airport: Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar International Airport, Nagpur
location.nearest_metro: Subhash Nagar / nearby Nagpur Metro station; exact nearest entrance requires map verification
location.nearest_major_hospital: Wockhardt / Orange City-area major hospitals; on-campus health centre available
location.technology_ecosystem: Nagpur urban industry and MIHAN/SEZ ecosystem; smaller technology market than major metros
location.cost_of_living_band: moderate (map/student-market inference; not an official index)
location.data_origin: official address and railway distance; coordinates, travel times and ecosystem are map-derived/inferred

senior_junior_culture.summary: The reviewed public sources do not provide enough independent first-person evidence to generalise about mentorship, club hierarchy or batch interaction.
positive_themes: large residential campus can enable peer interaction, but this is an inference
negative_themes: current mentorship and fresher-inclusion evidence is sparse

academic_culture.summary: Public discussion is too limited to establish attendance or grading strictness across departments. The institute has a research-oriented academic environment, while student posts focus more on placements than classroom experience.
positive_themes: research-project orientation mentioned in one discussion
negative_themes: department-level workload, attendance and grading remain unverified

hostel_reality.summary: A recent claimed-student comment describes first-year mess food as average but says hygiene and nutrition are monitored, while a 2025 visual tour shows rooms, mess and campus facilities. Current rules and experience can vary by block.
negative_themes: first-year mess taste described as average
current prohibited-item and curfew rules remain unclear

club_and_campus_life.summary: Official records list a broad technical, cultural and social-club ecosystem. Independent public evidence supports the existence of substantial campus infrastructure, but current activity intensity and selection fairness are not well documented.
positive_themes: visual evidence of a large campus
one 2026 response calls campus life good
negative_themes: activity status and fresher access not verified club by club
club_and_campus_life.evidence_strength: Strong institution-published signal; limited student-reported signal

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Several recent discussions criticise software-placement opportunity and coding culture relative to peer NITs, but some comments are hearsay or strongly personal. Official placement data should be used for outcomes, while these posts are treated as a cautionary qualitative signal only.
positive_themes: computing, cybersecurity and technical clubs are officially listed
students can pursue coding from non-CSE branches with individual preparation
negative_themes: repeated criticism of coding culture and software-opportunity breadth
branch and company eligibility may constrain non-CSE access
coding_and_placement_culture.evidence_strength: Moderate repeated student-reported concern with important bias limitations

locality_and_student_life.summary: VNIT is within Nagpur city and has road, railway, airport and metro access. Student lifestyle evidence is limited, so affordability, evening transport and food variety should be checked personally.
positive_themes: railway and airport within roughly single-digit kilometres
negative_themes: exact last-mile metro convenience and late-evening transport not independently assessed
locality_and_student_life.evidence_strength: Moderate official/map-derived connectivity signal; limited student evidence

overall_satisfaction.summary: Public opinion is conflicting: campus infrastructure and campus life receive positive comments, while recent posts raise concerns about coding culture and placement competitiveness. The evidence is not strong enough to state a college-wide satisfaction level.

parent_mode_summary.summary: VNIT Nagpur is a centrally funded Institute of National Importance with a large residential campus, established programmes and official national-ranking/placement evidence. Families should verify the final 2026-27 hostel and mess cost, exact hostel entitlement, current scholarship deadlines and programme-level placement data before deciding.

priorities.cost: Published 2026 academic charges and remission rules are captured; hostel/mess totals remain a gap.
priorities.placement_stability: NIRF provides comparable UG placement data for three years; the institute also reports 692 offers in 2024-25, which is not the same as unique students placed.
priorities.travel: Urban Nagpur location with railway, airport and metro access.
priorities.medical_and_hostel: On-campus health and broad hostel facilities are institution-published; current policies require verification.
priorities.source_confidence: Cutoffs, seat matrix and NIRF data are authoritative; qualitative experience is weaker.

student_mode_summary.summary: VNIT offers a broad branch mix, technical/cultural clubs and an urban residential campus. Students targeting software roles from non-CSE branches should verify current recruiter eligibility and prepare independently; recent public discussions raise concerns about coding culture, but the evidence is anecdotal and mixed.

priorities.branch_fit: Compare the specific branch curriculum and historical cutoff, not only institute brand.
priorities.coding_and_core: Official clubs support technical work; company eligibility and peer coding environment should be checked with current students.
priorities.campus_life: Large campus and many listed clubs, but activity status varies.
priorities.hostel: First-year twin sharing and later single-room arrangements are institution-published; lived experience varies.
priorities.internships: Official profile reports more than 105 internship companies in 2024-25, without a student internship count.

decision_risks.risk: A software-focused student choosing a core branch may face company-eligibility constraints.
decision_risks.basis: Recruiter-specific branch eligibility is not publicly complete; student discussions raise concerns about software opportunities.

decision_risks.risk: The final residential cost cannot yet be calculated reliably.
decision_risks.basis: Current hostel and mess fee details were not fully accessible.

decision_risks.risk: The 2024-25 figure of 692 is job offers, not confirmed unique students placed.
decision_risks.basis: Institution profile wording differs from NIRF placement-cohort metrics.

decision_risks.risk: Newer programmes have limited historical cutoff and placement evidence.
decision_risks.basis: Several 2026 programmes do not appear in the 2023-25 historical branch set.

decision_risks.risk: Scholarship eligibility and deadlines may change annually.
decision_risks.basis: Only broad fee-remission rules and government portals are verified; awards are never guaranteed.

decision_risks.risk: Current club activity and hostel rules may differ from static listings.
decision_risks.basis: Most official club/facility information is not dated activity evidence.

data_gaps: Final detailed 2026-27 hostel, mess, deposit and utility charges were not reliably accessible.
Current NAAC grade/validity and programme-wise NBA accreditation validity require authoritative manual verification.
Final 2024-25 and 2025-26 unique-student placement denominators, branch-wise placement rates, packages, internship counts and PPO counts were not fully accessible.
Recruiter-specific roles, eligible branches, minimum CGPA, selection counts and packages are largely unpublished.
Current hostel entitlement, curfew, room-allotment exceptions and prohibited-item rules require confirmation.
Current scholarship deadlines, award counts and donor-scheme benefits require active notices.
Club-by-club current activity, recruitment process and latest achievements are not consistently published.
Map-derived metro, hospital and travel-time fields require manual route verification.
Student-experience evidence is limited, self-selected and sometimes hearsay.

conflicts.source_2_value: NIRF reports unique students placed for earlier graduating cohorts
conflicts.possible_reason: Different year and metric: offers can exceed unique placed students.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Obtain the final 2024-25 placement report and preserve both offer and unique-student metrics.

conflicts.source_1_value: 2026 JoSAA seat matrix totals 1,143 seats across 14 programmes
conflicts.source_2_value: NIRF submission lists historical UG four-year sanctioned intake of 855
conflicts.possible_reason: Different years and programme scope; 2026 includes new programmes and B.Arch.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Do not compare as same-year values; retain year and programme scope.

conflicts.source_1_value: First-year twin sharing and later single rooms described
conflicts.source_2_value: Detailed current allotment/guarantee terms not verified
conflicts.possible_reason: Profile describes typical arrangement, not a legally guaranteed entitlement.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Confirm current hostel allotment circular for the incoming batch.

conflicts.source_2_value: Negative or cautious comments on coding culture and placement opportunities
conflicts.possible_reason: Different branches, years, expectations and self-selection bias.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Interview multiple current students from the target branch and compare with official branch-wise data.

conflicts.source_1_value: 2026 academic fee and one-time charge available
conflicts.source_2_value: Detailed 2026-27 hostel document not reliably extracted
conflicts.possible_reason: Academic and hostel fees are published separately.
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Verify hostel, mess, deposits and utilities before calculating total cost.

manual_verification_required: Confirm exact official college name, institute code 230 and main campus identity.
Confirm all 14 programme names and the category-wise 2026 seat matrix against the live JoSAA profile.
Spot-check JoSAA 2023-25 and CSAB 2025 cutoff rows across branches, categories, quotas and gender pools.
Confirm current NAAC and programme-wise NBA validity from authoritative certificates.
Download and verify the final 2026-27 academic, hostel, mess, deposit and utility fee circulars.
Confirm remission workflow and required income/category/PwD documents.
Obtain final branch-wise placement reports for 2023-24, 2024-25 and 2025-26 and reconcile offers versus unique students.
Confirm recruiter year, role, eligible branches, CGPA threshold, selection count and package before displaying company-specific claims.
Confirm current hostel guarantee, allotment rules, curfew and prohibited items.
Confirm active scholarships, amounts, deadlines, documents and renewal conditions.
Confirm current activity and recruitment for every listed club.
Verify station/airport/metro/hospital distances and travel times on a current map.
Review qualitative claims with multiple current students from the intended branch.
Check all URLs and replace any moved official documents.
